title: External MCP Integrations (Jira + Graphify)
summary: Module 09 integrates Jira and Graphify into Coodra with one shared pattern: wire the external system's own MCP server into the agent's config, let the agent call it, and have Coodra add only thin fusion that ties the external system to Coodra's own records. Coodra builds no Jira REST client and no Graphify graph reader, and mints no Feature Packs from either system. Track 9A (Jira) = Direct (ADR-016): coodra jira enable writes Atlassian's Rovo MCP server (mcp.atlassian.com/v1/mcp/authv2, Streamable HTTP, per-user OAuth 2.1); fusion is Link (Run<->issue via runs.issueRef, zero migration) + on-request write-back (the agent posts the Context Pack summary via Rovo's addCommentToJiraIssue). No Epic->Feature Pack transform. Track 9B (Graphify) = Option C, query-only (ADR-010 / ADR-015): coodra graphify enable writes Graphify's own stdio MCP server (python -m graphify.serve); the agent calls query_graph / get_node / get_neighbors / shortest_path. The dead query_codebase_graph tool + graphify.ts reader, and the later seed_feature_packs_from_graph + build_codebase_graph + get_feature_pack structure block, are all retired. A shared 9-Core substrate (MCP-config writers, /settings/integrations page, coodra <x> enable/disable/status CLI) is built once. Coodra's MCP manifest stays at 15 tools (Direct adds 0-1). Supersedes the build-our-own Jira design in system-architecture.md section 22 (ADR-016) and the original ADR-010 Graphify reader/importer (ADR-015).
supersedes: system-architecture.md section 22 — build-our-own Jira REST client + OAuth app + webhooks
Original ADR-010 / section 17 — Coodra-owned Graphify graph.json reader + community importer
